title: Round decimals using a number line
description: Practice using a number line to round decimal numbers.
topic_path: Round decimals using a number line

assessment_items.question_content:
Answer two questions about the number $1.176$ below.

[[☃ image 1]]

1. **Which point is at $1.176$ on the number line?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

2. **What is $1.176$ rounded to the nearest tenth?**   

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
Point $\maroonE{\text{B}}$ shows $\maroonE{1.176}$ on the number line.

[[☃ image 2]]

Now, let's look at the $\goldE{\text{tenths}}$.   

[[☃ image 2]]

$\maroonE{1.176}$ is between $\goldE{1.1}$ and $\goldE{1.2}$ but closer to $\goldE{1.2}$.  So, $\maroonE{1.176}$ rounds up to $\goldE{1.2}$.

Point $\maroonE{\text{B}}$ shows $\maroonE{1.176}$ on the number line.

$\maroonE{1.176}$ rounded to the nearest tenth is $\goldE{1.2}$.

options.alt: A vertical number line labeled 1 to 2 with tick marks every one tenth unit. Point A appears slightly below tick mark 1.1. Point B appears slightly below tick mark 1.2. Point C appears slightly below tick mark 1.8.

assessment_items.question_content:
Answer two questions about the number $44.197$ below.

[[☃ image 1]]

1. **Which point is at $44.197$ on the number line?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

2. **What is $44.197$ rounded to the nearest whole number?**   

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
Point $\blueE{\text{C}}$ shows $\blueE{44.197}$ on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, let's look at whole numbers, which are the $\goldE{\text{ones}}$.

[[☃ image 2]]

$\blueE{44.197}$ is between $\goldE{44}$ and $\goldE{45}$ but closer to $\goldE{44}$.  So, $\blueE{44.197}$ rounds down to $\goldE{44}$.

Point $\blueE{\text{C}}$ shows $\blueE{44.197}$ on the number line.

$\blueE{44.197}$ rounded to the nearest whole number is $\goldE{44}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 40 to 50 with tick marks every one unit. Point A appears slightly below tick mark 41. Point B appears slightly below tick mark 44. Point C appears slightly above tick mark 44.

assessment_items.question_content:
Answer two questions about point $\maroonE{\text{A}}$ on the number line below.

[[☃ image 2]]

1. **What is $\maroonE{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest hundredth?**   

[[☃ input-number 1]]

2. **What is $\maroonE{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest tenth?**   

[[☃ input-number 2]]
hints:
First, let's look at the $\blueE{\text{hundredths}}$.   

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonE{\text{A}}$ is between $\blueE{7.51}$ and $\blueE{7.52}$ but closer to $\blueE{7.51}$.  So, $\maroonE{\text{A}}$ rounds down to $\blueE{7.51}$.

Next, let's look at the $\goldE{\text{tenths}}$.   

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonE{\text{A}}$ is between $\goldE{7.5}$ and $\goldE{7.6}$ but closer to $\goldE{7.5}$.  So, $\maroonE{\text{A}}$ rounds down to $\goldE{7.5}$.

$\maroonE{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest hundredth is $\blueE{7.51}$.

$\maroonE{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest tenth is $\goldE{7.5}$.

options.alt: A vertical number line labeled 7.5 to 7.6 with tick marks every one hundredth unit. Point A appears slightly above tick mark 7.51.
